pi_items.source: AirNav (https://www.airnav.com/airport/KRHV), Wikipedia, and FAA records confirm KRHV is the ICAO code for Reid-Hillview Airport of Santa Clara County, California
AirNav (http://www.airnav.com/airport/kmod), Wikipedia, and FAA records confirm KMOD is the ICAO code for Modesto City-County Airport (Harry Sham Field), Modesto, California
GPX Standard Documentation at topografix.com (https://www.topografix.com/gpx/1/1/), GPS Exchange Format specification confirms <gpx> is the required root XML element in all GPX files

Task setup and export scripts expect this filename. Web search of Avare documentation and GitHub repository found no evidence that 'route_export.gpx' is Avare's default export filename. Task description states 'Save the file as route_export', suggesting this is a task-specified requirement, not an Avare convention.

privileged_info_summary: The task creates a flight plan between two real US airports: KRHV (Reid-Hillview Airport of Santa Clara County, California) and KMOD (Modesto City-County Airport, California). The output is a GPX file (GPS Exchange Format), which is a real, standardized XML format for GPS data where all valid GPX files must use <gpx> as the root XML element. The task uses the real Avare aviation application. The specific export filename 'route_export.gpx' is specified by the task but could not be verified as Avare's standard export naming convention.
